relationships.description: Phosphorus application significantly increased protein content
Protein is a nutrient content of finger millet grain
Calcium is a nutrient content of finger millet grain
Iron is a nutrient content of finger millet grain
Zinc is a nutrient content of finger millet grain
U-15 is a variety of finger millet
P-224 is a variety of finger millet
Local variety is a variety of finger millet
U-15 variety exhibited the highest protein content at specific phosphorus levels
P-224 variety had the highest calcium content across all sites
P-224 variety had the highest iron content at Kakamega
Local varieties had the highest zinc content across all sites
